name: architecture_hosts_example_system
description: Architecture-doc prose modeled on USD00050013_Rev_B Example System Cybersecurity Architecture.docx — service identifiers (LDAP/SAML IdP/SIEM), zones (DMZ, mgmt VLAN), and vendor products. Architecture docs are typically less host-dense than SSPs but richer in named services; this case pins that signal.

fixture_docs.title: Example System Cybersecurity Architecture Rev B
fixture_docs.text:
## 3.2 Identity and access services

The Example System enclave consumes three centrally-operated identity services:

  1. LDAP directory (read-only consumer of ldap.example.local)
  2. SAML 2.0 IdP — Okta — serves browser SSO for the operator console
  3. PIV-CAC smart-card auth via the enterprise ActivClient agent

Log aggregation flows to the enterprise Splunk cluster (siem.example.local) over TCP/9997. Endpoint protection is CrowdStrike Falcon; vulnerability scans are run from Tenable.sc with the Example-managed Nessus scanners.

## 4.1 Network zones

  - DMZ:          external-facing reverse proxies
  - mgmt VLAN:    out-of-band administrative network (jump hosts only)
  - prod enclave: production application + database servers
  - lab enclave:  developer integration testing, segregated

All inter-zone traffic traverses the Palo Alto PA-5260 boundary firewall pair.
